data.body:
=== UPDATE: coaching-posture research + final decisions + v4 plan-of-record ===

COACHING-POSTURE RESEARCH (verdict): the "require-basics(deterministic)-then-advise / positive-guidance" posture
is well-supported by 2026 research. KEYSTONE: LLMs can't reliably FIND their own errors but CAN fix them once
given the LOCATION (Tyen et al. ACL'24, 2311.08516) -> the reviewer's job is to SUPPLY the finding+location the
agent can't self-detect; the agent is a competent fixer from there. External feedback beats intrinsic
self-critique (2310.01798, 2402.08115, LLM-Modulo 2402.01817). Rigid SEMANTIC gates get churned/reward-hacked
(SpecBench 2605.21384) -> keep the hard floor MINIMAL+unambiguous; make every semantic judgment ADVISORY.
FEEDBACK-AUTHORING SPEC (per advisory finding): (1) GROUND it in a criterion/file/section (highest-value lever;
in-place located feedback ~doubles fix accuracy vs free-text); (2) SPECIFIC+ACTIONABLE not generic; (3) a
concrete SUGGESTED edit ONLY when confident (a wrong fix regresses BELOW none, 2504.06939); (4) SEVERITY-RANK +
CAP (few prioritized, critical first); (5) AFFIRM what passes (behavior-specific) to suppress over-correction/
churn (FlipFlop: pushback after a CORRECT answer flips ~46%); (6) express as a CHECKLIST + require addressing
each (TICK/STICK 2410.03608, Rubrics-as-Rewards 2507.17746). HONEST GAP: no direct measurement of how often
advisory feedback is IGNORED (advisory-vs-blocking is reasoned design, not measured) -> exactly what our
FP-instrumentation measures on our own data (remediation-rate-per-criterion).

DECISIONS THIS ROUND (Joe's pushback accepted):
- PER-CRITERIA BLOCKING simplified to what we can build honestly: each Layer-2 criterion config = advisory
  (default) | blocking-with-`--force`-override. We do NOT build a true no-override hard gate; a project composes
  one in CI by checking for the signed result (we expose the data; not recommended). DROPPED (-> follow-ons): the
  measured-FP-surfacing guardrail (it only measures --force circumvention, not real FP/remediation; needs tooling
  + human feedback), auto-demote (same), and the confidence/severity-floor for blocking (LLMs bad at self-
  confidence; "can't meet an AC" isn't the only critical defect; fine-tune later on usage data).
- INCOMPLETE-REVIEW knob: outcome INDETERMINATE (not failed - cap is external; not certified - process didn't
  finish; no signature). Whether INDETERMINATE blocks `claim` is a PROJECT KNOB; DEFAULT = non-blocking (cert
  records "incomplete"; claim proceeds with that on record).
- CHUNKING: determined EMPIRICALLY (NOT from DSO's rubric - what we measure is materially different). Run
  chunk-size experiments, N repeats each, find the quality-degradation point; prime with DSO tickets as
  realistic inputs.
- SIGNALS are validated+improved, not trusted: file_impact empty / AC absent -> COACH the agent to add/repair,
  don't penalize. Standardize on `## Acceptance Criteria` across ALL types (stories' DoD / epics' success-
  criteria collapse into ACs). The DSO complexity label is UNVALIDATED evaluator OUTPUT - one input among many,
  never ground truth.
- DSO data role: NOT a threshold source (rebar != DSO; DSO over-tuned to itself). Use it as (a) representative
  sample tickets for "how do we review THIS kind of ticket" discussions, (b) a SIMULATION corpus to test how
  candidate rules redistribute tickets across review levels, (c) priming for the chunk/threshold experiments.
- ROUTING on complexity + has_children (NOT type): rebar reviews tickets from MANY creation styles that are
  inconsistently typed; DSO's strict type discipline is one creation style, not the only one. Anything ported
  from DSO must be Python, polyglot, and a general principle.
- COACHING dual goals: (1) positive guidance to author better plans (require deterministic basics, then advise);
  (2) the signed cert = a composable "rigorous agentic dev vs vibe-coding" signal for CI (review +
  completion-verification + future workflow signatures).

OBSERVABILITY / FP-ANALYSIS SUBSTRATE (cheap, IN-SCOPE; the enabler for the FP follow-ons): decouple cheap
CAPTURE now from offline ANALYSIS later. STORAGE MODEL DECIDED: (a) ATTESTATION/SIGNATURE = a normal reduced
event ON the ticket (head state; what claim checks; reuses signing.py). (b) REVIEW_RESULT (bulky observability:
per-criterion verdicts + FINDING FINGERPRINTS [hash(criterion+normalized location+normalized text)] + model/chunk/
budget/depth metadata + captured-but-untrusted self-confidence + override justifications) = a reducer-IGNORED
SIDECAR EVENT on the ticket (free sync/push + conflict-free union-merge + co-located with the events the analysis
must correlate against: EDIT/STATUS/reopen/completion-verification), NOT folded into head/graph/health (the
session_log out-of-hot-paths treatment at the event-type level; one new bit = reducer skips this type). REJECTED:
single shared ledger file (merge conflicts in a multi-clone auto-pushed store; breaks per-event-file union-merge).
ALTERNATIVE/fallback only if ticket-dir growth bites: a separate per-review-FILE store / `reviews` branch
(conflict-safe, pristine ticket dirs, but a new store + cross-store join). Body kept COMPACT (full plan text
recoverable from the bound commit, never duplicated); old REVIEW_RESULTs compactable/archivable after extraction.
OFFLINE replay+join on ticket_id+fingerprints yields per-criterion fire-rate, REMEDIATION-rate (fingerprint
resolved after a revision = the real "is the advice useful" metric), FP proxy (flagged->overridden/ignored->
ticket closes clean), FN proxy (silent->later reworked), chunk-size quality, confidence-vs-correctness,
depth-tier distribution. Capture only; never aggregate/decide in-system; no human-feedback requirement.

ARTIFACT: plan-of-record-v4.md written (DESIGN OF RECORD; supersedes our-review-dimensions-v3.md), appended to
this log immediately after this entry. Contains: purpose/posture, two-layer architecture, verdict+blocking model,
the feedback-authoring spec, the criteria registry, execution (chunking/tooling/proportionate scrutiny),
convergence, attestation/cert, the observability substrate + storage model, tuning methodology, the FOLLOW-ON
REGISTER (F1-F12, tracked separately), and open/pending-approval items.

NEXT: pending Joe's approval of (proportionate-depth posture + thresholds, budget posture, bug-skip+follow-on),
decompose the v4 plan into the rebar implementation epic (one wave, tasks with deps) tracked in rebar, and create
the follow-on register items F1-F12 as separate linked tickets (discovered_from).
